findings.title: Password storage uses a fast, unsalted SHA-256 hash rather than an approved password KDF.
Password verification uses a non-constant-time equality comparison.
Successful reset-token verification does not consume the token, allowing replay until expiry.

reasoning: All repository files were inspected. The reset route is rate-limited before registration and returns a generic message. Reset tokens use randomBytes and are compared with timingSafeEqual, but the issued used flag is never changed after successful verification. No development bypass branch was found.
